name: Hilltop inflation
summary: Slow-roll inflation occurs near a local maximum of the inflaton potential, where the field rolls slowly downhill. The 'hilltop' class is the generic small-field inflation regime: inflaton excursions stay sub-Planckian, predictions are r << 0.01 (tiny tensor-to-scalar ratio), and n_s ~ 0.95-0.97 emerges from slow-roll near the maximum. Easily accommodates Planck + BICEP/Keck data; the small field range avoids weak-gravity-conjecture concerns that beset large-field models.

addresses_puzzles.note: Predicts r ~ 10^-4 - 10^-3 (well below current bounds), n_s ~ 0.96. Initial conditions question is sharper than for large-field models since the inflaton must start near the top of the potential, but anthropic / eternal-inflation arguments restore generic-initial-condition viability.

touches_benchmarks.note: Generic prediction r < 0.002 for monotonically-steepening potential, below LiteBIRD / CMB-S4 sensitivity in most parameter space.
n_s = 1 - 2/(p N) for V ~ V_0 (1 - (phi/mu)^p); specific small-field potentials match Planck.

introduces.note: Hilltop height V_0, scale mu, power p in V ~ V_0(1 - (phi/mu)^p).

history: Boubekeur and Lyth (2005), 'Hilltop inflation', JCAP 07, 010, arXiv:hep-ph/0502047 — defined and analysed the small-field hilltop class. Among the leading inflationary frameworks consistent with Planck + BICEP/Keck data; weak-gravity-conjecture pressure is minimal.
